site.name: Ludoratory
headline: From Experiment
to Experience
site.thesis: A laboratory adapting board, card, and party games into multi-device experiences. Browser-native. Real-time. No install. Bring your own phones; we'll supply the chaos.
site.warning: ~ side effects may include excessive game-night planning ~

games.name: Krimi
games.description: Decode the forensic scientist's clues to unmask the hidden murderer. A social-deduction party game played on a shared screen, controlled from your phones.

games.name: Colorlition
games.description: A real-time card-drafting party game where political coalitions are built one demand at a time. Don't let your top three colors flip-flop.

games.name: Unmatched
games.description: Zero duplicates. Total cooperation. A web-based word game where great minds mustn't think alike. Friends, a shared screen, your phones.

games.name: Arcane Poker
games.description: What happens when the Tarot rewrites the rules of Poker? A tactical evolution where every Page drawn bends the board. Your fate is on the table.

apparatus.name: react-gameroom
apparatus.description: The reusable lobby system every Ludoratory game runs on. A stateless React component library handling player slots, room state, QR-code joining, and URL routing. Bring your own state, bring your own game.

houseRules.title: Free to play, always.
houseRules.description: No paywalls, no subscriptions, no premium tiers. You and your friends, a URL, and a phone each.

houseRules.title: Open source, every game.
houseRules.description: Each game's code lives on GitHub. Read it, fork it, run your own copy. Mistakes welcome, PRs even more so.

houseRules.title: No analytics, no trackers.
houseRules.description: No third-party scripts watching what you do here. Your game-night is yours. We don't even know how many people played.

houseRules.title: The database wipes daily.
houseRules.description: Every room evaporates at midnight UTC. There is no archive, no leaderboard server, no record. Play, then disappear.
